behavior_in_task_description.reason: All test-asserted file paths (packages/trigger-sdk/src/v3/shared.ts, packages/trigger-sdk/src/v3/tasks.ts, references/v3-catalog/src/trigger/sdkUsage.ts, .cursor/rules/writing-tasks.mdc, docs/triggering.mdx), function/method names (triggerAndPoll, trigger, batchTrigger, triggerAndWait, batchTriggerAndWait), and the string 'triggerAndPoll' are all explicitly named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md describes what needs to be removed and from which files, but does not specify exact line numbers, sed commands, or replacement text. A developer must still reason about how to make the changes.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for absence of 'triggerAndPoll' in specific files, presence of other methods, valid syntax, and balanced imports — all behavioral properties that any correct removal would satisfy regardless of implementation approach (e.g., different sed patterns, manual editing, etc.).

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most fail_to_pass tests (test_triggerandpoll_function_removed, test_triggerandpoll_not_exported, test_sdk_usage_no_triggerandpoll, test_cursor_rules_no_triggerandpoll, test_docs_triggering_no_triggerandpoll) are essentially regex-based text searches on file contents via Node.js (e.g., /triggerAndPoll/.test(content)). While some use regex to parse exports, none actually import/execute the SDK module or test runtime behavior. They are effectively 'assert string in file.read()' checks wrapped in No

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail_to_pass tests check for the absence of 'triggerAndPoll' in files that currently contain it. A stub or no-op change would leave the original code intact and fail these tests. The agent must actively remove the function and its references.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All requirements from instruction.md are tested: function removal from shared.ts, removal from tasks export, removal from example code, removal from documentation files (.cursor/rules and docs/triggering.mdx), and preservation of other methods.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (e.g., test_triggerandpoll_function_removed, test_other_task_methods_preserved), have docstrings explaining what they check, and are annotated with [static]/[pr_diff] and pass_to_pass/fail_to_pass categories.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task involves code/documentation removal, not structured data production.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, function names, and commands in instruction.md and tests are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly lists exact file paths including .cursor/rules/writing-tasks.mdc and docs/triggering.mdx under 'Files to Look At', which removes the need for the agent to localize where documentation references exist. This is borderline but acceptable for the main SDK files; however, listing the cursor rules file and docs file is leakage since discovering all documentation references is part of the task.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is primarily a code and documentation removal task, not a config-edit task.
